opp.name: Bekaert_S2P
opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is pure traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, iSave, iSource, iSupplier) with zero AI modules ‚Äî no Merlin, no Merlin Intake, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze. This is a process/coverage deal targeting the breadth of the S2P suite without any AI-specific module selected. The complete absence of AI SKUs is itself a strong negative signal.
• Qualified stage with BD_Engagement Unengaged. Zero Avoma meetings returned in the searched window. Last activity Apr 16, 2026 suggests some recent touch but no recorded conversations from which to extract buyer dialogue. There is no captured rep-buyer transcript to ground any AI sentiment from.
• Medium confidence: product mix alone is a strong negative signal ‚Äî the breadth of traditional S2P modules without any AI module indicates this is a coverage-driven deal, not an AI-driven one. SF Description is null and no Avoma data. The complete absence of any AI module in scope grounds the AI Resistant assessment at 2.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, ROI target, or savings case is documented in Salesforce or call transcripts. The available evidence is limited to the product scope (full S2P suite: iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRisk) which signals broad procurement transformation intent but carries no stated pain owner, cost baseline, cycle-time improvement target, or financial impact. With zero buyer calls discovered and no Description or business-objective fields populated, the underlying business driver and quantified value case are genuinely unknown. The deal cannot progress credibly past Qualified without articulating and validating the business case with the buyer.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Patrick Van Hecke (DM), Vlad Tomita (DM)
No call/task evidence of advocacy or engagement
champion.narrative: No champion is identified or developed. The two Decision Maker contact roles (Patrick Van Hecke, Vlad Tomita) are potential champion candidates given their Procurement/Purchasing Director titles, but there is zero evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or active engagement: no calls, no tasks, no emails logged in the trailing 90 days. A champion by definition provides access to power, shares internal intel, and drives the buying process on our behalf; none of that is visible here. The deal has been silent for 71 days, which suggests either the contact has disengaged or the relationship was never strong enough to generate momentum. Champion-building is the immediate priority before any other MEDDPICC element can be validated.

competition.narrative: Coupa and GEP Worldwide are named as competitors in the Salesforce Competitors__c field, but no call transcript, next-step note, or task provides context on how Bekaert is evaluating them, whether this is an active bake-off or a passive shortlist, or what the competitive wedge should be. With zero buyer calls discovered, the competitive dynamics—who is ahead, what Coupa and GEP are pitching, what Bekaert values in each vendor, and how Zycus is differentiated—are all unknown. The competing-against fact is confirmed (partial status), but the competitive strategy and positioning are a gap. Without re-engagement and discovery, Zycus is flying blind against two strong, well-funded rivals.

sources: Product scope inferred but not buyer-confirmed
identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain Bekaert is trying to solve is unknown. No buyer has articulated on a call or in writing what is broken in their current procurement stack, what manual workarounds they are enduring, what compliance or spend-visibility gap they face, or what business outcome (cost savings, cycle-time reduction, supplier consolidation, risk mitigation) they are targeting. The product scope (full S2P suite) implies dissatisfaction with a fragmented or underperforming procurement system, but this is inferred from the solution scope, not confirmed by the buyer. Pain-related Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c) are empty or unread. Without a named pain owner or a single discovery call, the pain is a hypothesis, not a validated fact.

sources: No calls, no contract/legal field data
Account contacts include legal counsel but not opp-linked
paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are completely unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement-channel discussion, or SI/partner involvement is documented. The account contact list includes legal counsel (Florian Finke, Maxime Vanhie) who would typically be involved in contract redlining for a $250k SaaS deal, but there is no evidence they are engaged or aware of this opportunity. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is empty, so whether Bekaert has an incumbent contract forcing function or is starting fresh is unclear. With the deal cold for 71 days and still in Qualified stage, no contracting discussion has occurred.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Patrick Van Hecke (DM), Vlad Tomita (DM)
Account contacts (not opp-linked): CPO, VP Finance, VP Procurement
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is confirmed. The opportunity carries two Decision Maker contact roles (Patrick Van Hecke, Procurement; Vlad Tomita, Purchasing Director EMEA) but neither has been engaged on a recorded call, and no task or field evidence shows budget authority or commercial conversation. The account roster includes senior finance and procurement executives (Luc Vankemmelbeke, CPO; Fernando Rodriguez, VP Global Procurement Operations; Frederic Peeters, VP Finance) who would typically control a $250k S2P investment, but none are linked to this opportunity as contact roles and there is no evidence they are aware of or sponsoring this deal. Without a single buyer conversation in 71 days, budget authority and approval chain are completely unmapped.

sources: Account-level exec roster suggests Global/EMEA complexity but unconfirmed
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unmapped. No call sequence, stakeholder escalation pattern, approval-chain discussion, or buying-committee structure is visible. The two Decision Maker contact roles (Van Hecke, Tomita) suggest a dual-DM setup or regional/functional split, but their relationship to each other, to the broader buying committee, and to budget approval is unconfirmed. Whether this is a centralized Global Procurement decision (likely given the CPO and VP Global Procurement on the account) or a regional EMEA play led by Tomita is unclear. No field evidence (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c) is captured, and with 71 days of silence the real process—if one exists—is invisible.

sources: SF synthesis-map fields empty or unread
Product scope signals intent but not criteria
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are unknown. No RFP, RFI, BRD, evaluation scorecard, or use-case validation is documented in Salesforce or call transcripts. The product scope (full S2P suite) implies broad functional requirements across sourcing, contract, supplier, procurement, invoicing, and risk modules, but how Bekaert will evaluate fit, what their must-haves are, and how they weight Zycus against Coupa and GEP is unconfirmed. With zero discovery or demo calls on record and no fields populated (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, or AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are all empty or unread), the criteria that will determine vendor selection are a black box.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 2026-03-20, moved to Qualified stage the same day, and has been static for 98 days. Last Salesforce activity 2026-04-16 (71 days ago). No field-history changes to Stage, Amount, CloseDate, or ForecastCategory in trailing 90 days. No Tasks or Events logged in trailing 90 days. Next_Step__c is empty. The deal is cold.

items.detail: Close date 2026-12-31 (188 days out) is not credible for a deal still in Qualified stage with zero discovery, no EB, no champion, and 71 days of silence. No realistic path from Qualified to close exists without immediate re-engagement and multi-month enterprise sales cycle (discovery, demo, RFP, shortlist, commercials, contract).

No Economic Buyer confirmed and no exec-level engagement (CPO, VP Finance, VP Procurement on account but not opp-linked). $250k S2P investment requires C-level or senior VP sign-off; without EB access the deal cannot progress past Qualified.

71 days of buyer silence signals either the initiative is paused/cancelled, the contact has left or been reassigned, or Zycus was never a serious contender and the opp was created prematurely. High risk of ghost deal.

Coupa and GEP are strong, well-funded competitors with established EMEA presence. Without discovery or demo activity, Zycus has not activated its competitive wedge (Merlin AI, modular flexibility, price) and is likely invisible or forgotten in the buyer's evaluation.

Close date 2026-12-31 is 188 days out. Zero calls discovered, zero validated buyer engagement since mid-April. No recent next step logged. Stage velocity is zero; no path from Qualified to close in 188 days is credible without re-engagement.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains frozen in Qualified stage for 98 days with zero buyer engagement for 71 days. No calls, no champion activity, no path to close against Coupa and GEP. Early-stage deal lacks all fundamental elements.

flags: MULTI-THREAD TO POWER: Only 2 buyer-side contact roles on a $250k enterprise deal is a single-thread risk. If the deal reactivates, immediately map and engage the economic buyer tier—likely the CPO (Luc Vankemmelbeke), VP Finance (Frederic Peeters), or VP Global Procurement (Fernando Rodriguez)—and add them as contact roles. A Qualified-stage deal with no EB access and no champion advocacy will not progress to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected.
best_practice_check.summary: This deal is cold, under-qualified, and at extreme risk. Immediate re-engagement or disqualification is required.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa and GEP Worldwide are the named competitors per Salesforce (no mention date available, likely logged at or near opp creation 2026-03-20, ~98 days ago). With zero buyer calls and no competitive-context evidence in tasks, next steps, or free-text fields, threat levels and current status are unconfirmed. Both are credible enterprise S2P vendors and must be treated as active threats until proven otherwise. Coupa is typically the market-share leader and price-sensitive; GEP is the consulting-led, fit-for-complex-enterprise alternative. Without discovery or demo activity, Zycus's competitive wedge (likely Merlin AI negotiation and supplier intelligence, plus modular flexibility vs Coupa's rigidity) has not been activated. The single strongest current threat is Coupa by market presence, but this is an assumption in the absence of evidence.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin AI-powered negotiation and supplier analytics vs Coupa's rigid, expensive platform; modular S2P flexibility and faster deployment; undercut on price if Bekaert is cost-sensitive.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Out-innovate GEP on AI (Merlin agentic negotiation, I2O intelligence); emphasize SaaS-native speed and user experience vs GEP's consulting-heavy, slower approach; win on price and agility for EMEA regional deployment.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is unassessed. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all null or unread, and no Avoma calls exist to gauge buyer AI appetite or Merlin narrative resonance. Product scope includes iRisk and the full S2P suite, which could integrate Merlin negotiation and supplier intelligence, but whether Bekaert has articulated AI needs or responded to the agentic Merlin story is unknown.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations are unknown. No discovery, demo, or use-case validation has occurred. Product scope (full S2P suite) is logged but fit against Bekaert's functional, technical, and business requirements is unconfirmed.

This is a process/coverage deal targeting the breadth of the S2P suite without any AI-specific module selected. The complete absence of AI SKUs is itself a strong negative signal.
• Qualified stage with BD_Engagement Unengaged. Zero Avoma meetings returned in the searched window. Last activity Apr 16, 2026 suggests some recent touch but no recorded conversations from which to extract buyer dialogue. There is no captured rep-buyer transcript to ground any AI sentiment from.

deltas.to: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 71 days of buyer silence on a $250k S2P deal in early Qualified stage with 188 days to a placeholder close date is a failed
deltas.subject: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 71 days of buyer silence on a $250k S2P deal in early Qualified stage with 188 days to a placeholder close date is a failed qualification or a lost deal. The highest-leverage move is for the deal owner to reach out directly to both Decision Makers (Van Hecke, Tomita) this week with a crisp re-qualification question: is there a live S2P initiative, what is the timeline, and are we still in consideration. If no reply in 10 business days, escalate to the account's CPO or VP Procurement (if reachable) for a senior-to-senior check, or disqualify and close-lost to free capacity. Do not let this sit in Pipeline forecast another month.

deltas.to: ZERO DISCOVERY MEANS ZERO FOUNDATION: Without a single buyer call, the business case, pain, EB, criteria, process, champion, and competitive positioning are all
deltas.subject: ZERO DISCOVERY MEANS ZERO FOUNDATION: Without a single buyer call, the business case, pain, EB, criteria, process, champion, and competitive positioning are all unknown. If the deal reopens, the first action is a deep discovery call (not a demo) with the relevant stakeholder or Tomita to map the business problem, quantify the value case, identify the buying committee and EB, understand the decision process and timeline, and confirm Coupa/GEP evaluation status. Skipping discovery and jumping to demo or pricing on a cold re-engage will fail.
